message:
test(repair): add verification gate for adaptive refresh expected-loss improvement (bd-m5wf.4.5)

Add verification_gate_hybrid_expected_loss_improvement test with 5 parts:
1. Model selects optimal policy per workload profile (Idle/Light/Heavy/Burst)
2. Economic behavior: block-count wins at low write rates, age-only at high
3. Hybrid achieves >10% improvement under high-risk parameters (1e9 cost, 10% corruption)
4. Stale-window p95 reduction >30% (actual: 83.3%) for heavy writes
5. Decision boundary at 16.67 writes/sec with loss crossover within 1e-8

Close bd-m5wf.4.5 verification gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-m5wf.4.2","title":"Implement block-count staleness tracking per repair group","description":"## Purpose

Track the number of block writes per repair group since last symbol refresh. Trigger eager refresh when threshold exceeded.

## Scope

1. **Write counter** — Per-group atomic counter in RepairGroupStorage. Incremented on every committed block write to the group. Reset on symbol refresh.

2. **Threshold configuration** — Configurable block-count threshold (default: 1000 blocks). When counter exceeds threshold, schedule eager symbol refresh.

3. **Eager refresh trigger** — Hook into the repair pipeline to trigger immediate symbol refresh for groups exceeding threshold.

4. **Integration** — Wire write counter into ffs-mvcc commit path (post-commit hook increments group counters).

## Acceptance criteria
- Per-group write counter tracked
- Threshold-based eager refresh triggers correctly
- Counter reset after refresh
- Unit tests: counter increment, threshold trigger, reset
- E2E script: write N blocks, verify refresh triggered
- Structured logging for threshold events","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:57.045830254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T21:00:44.188801Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T21:00:44.185097Z","close_reason":"Implemented per-group write counter (writes_since_refresh) in GroupRefreshState, block_count_threshold configuration, BlockCountThreshold refresh mode, threshold-exceeded logging, counter reset on refresh, and public API (set_block_count_threshold, writes_since_refresh). All 334 ffs-repair tests pass, full workspace clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:57.045830254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:10.489391605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.4.3","title":"Implement hybrid refresh policy (age + block-count)","description":"## Purpose

Combine age-timeout and block-count triggers into a hybrid refresh policy. Runtime-configurable.

## Scope

1. **RefreshPolicy enum** — `AgeOnly(Duration)`, `BlockCount(u64)`, `Hybrid { age: Duration, block_count: u64 }`.

2. **Hybrid logic** — Refresh triggers when EITHER age timeout OR block-count threshold is exceeded. This is the expected-loss-optimal policy.

3. **CLI configuration** — `--refresh-policy age-only|block-count|hybrid` with threshold parameters.

4. **Runtime switching** — Policy can be changed without restart via signal or evidence command.

## Acceptance criteria
- RefreshPolicy enum with 3 variants
- Hybrid triggers on either condition
- CLI flags for policy selection
- Unit tests for each policy variant
- E2E script testing policy switching","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:58.000469060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T04:01:39.101950Z","closed_at":"2026-03-16T04:01:39.101229Z","close_reason":"Added RefreshPolicy::Hybrid variant with age+block-count dual-trigger. Hybrid checks both dirty_age >= max_staleness and writes_since_refresh >= block_count_threshold, refreshing on whichever fires first. Added HybridAge/HybridBlockCount/HybridScrub refresh modes. with_group_refresh_policy auto-wires block_count_threshold for Hybrid. Telemetry summary includes hybrid format string. All 336 ffs-repair tests pass, workspace clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:58.000469060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:11.598803512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","title":"Implement stale-window SLO instrumentation and alerting","description":"## Purpose

Track stale-window percentiles (time and block-count since last refresh per group) and alert when SLO is exceeded. This bead focuses on the SLO *policy* layer (thresholds, breach detection, alerting); raw repair metrics (scrub rate, decode success) are handled by bd-m5wf.8.4. Uses the metrics framework from bd-m5wf.8.1 for counter/gauge/histogram primitives.

## Scope

1. **Stale-window metrics** — Per-group tracking of:
   - Time since last symbol refresh (uses Gauge from bd-m5wf.8.1)
   - Blocks written since last refresh (uses Counter from bd-m5wf.8.1)
   - p50/p95/p99 across all groups (uses Histogram from bd-m5wf.8.1)

2. **SLO definition** — Configurable SLO: max acceptable stale window (default: p95 < 60s OR p95 < 5000 blocks). SLO configuration via RepairConfig struct.

3. **Alert emission** — Structured log marker `repair_stale_window_slo_breach` when any group exceeds SLO. Does NOT duplicate the raw metric emission from bd-m5wf.8.4; only emits policy-level breach events.

4. **Evidence integration** — `ffs evidence --preset repair-freshness` shows per-group staleness.

## Acceptance criteria
- Stale-window percentiles tracked via bd-m5wf.8.1 histogram primitives
- SLO breach detected and logged (policy-level, not raw metrics)
- Evidence preset for repair freshness
- Unit tests for SLO threshold logic and percentile-based breach detection
- E2E script validating SLO alerting fires when stale windows exceed threshold","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:58.945075625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T13:13:39.467193Z","closed_at":"2026-03-16T13:13:39.466599Z","close_reason":"Implemented StaleWindowSlo config (max_age_ms, max_writes, percentile), SloEvaluation with percentile-based breach detection, evaluate_slo method on ScrubWithRecovery that emits repair_stale_window_slo_breach structured log on breach. 7 unit tests (boundary cases, age breach, writes breach, percentile-below, empty groups). All 342 ffs-repair tests pass, workspace clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logging","observability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:58.945075625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:12.691667492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T06:09:54.043823158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5","title":"EPIC: Multi-Host Repair Coordination and Distributed Symbol Exchange","description":"## Purpose

Extend repair from single-host only (V1.1) to multi-host coordination. Deferred from V1.1 OQ5: repair symbols remain on same device, single-host repair ownership via coordination records.

## Scope

### Sub-beads

1. **Design: multi-host repair protocol** — Define the protocol for multiple hosts to coordinate repair symbol generation, exchange, and ownership. Consensus-free design (no distributed consensus needed for repair — use optimistic ownership with conflict detection).

2. **Implement repair ownership transfer** — Allow ownership of a repair group to transfer between hosts. Coordination record with lease-based ownership (TTL). Stale ownership detection and recovery.

3. **Implement symbol exchange protocol** — Mechanism for hosts to exchange repair symbols for groups they don't own. Read-only symbol retrieval from remote host. Asupersync-based transport (no tokio).

4. **Implement distributed repair pipeline** — End-to-end multi-host repair: detect corruption on host A, retrieve symbols from host B, decode and verify on host A.

5. **Verification gate** — Multi-host repair correctness, ownership transfer safety, no split-brain.

## Non-goals
- Distributed filesystem (each host mounts independently)
- Real-time replication (repair is for recovery, not replication)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:39.045968821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:39.045968821Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","design","durability","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:39.045968821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","title":"Design multi-host repair protocol with optimistic ownership","description":"## Purpose

Design the protocol for multi-host repair coordination. Must be consensus-free and use optimistic ownership with conflict detection.

## Scope

1. **Ownership model** — Lease-based ownership per repair group. Host acquires ownership by writing a coordination record with TTL. Ownership expired → any host can claim.

2. **Conflict detection** — If two hosts claim ownership simultaneously, detect via coordination record version mismatch. Resolution: lower host-id wins (deterministic tiebreak).

3. **Symbol exchange** — Read-only retrieval of repair symbols from a remote host's storage. Protocol: request(group_id, generation) → response(symbols[]).

4. **Transport** — Asupersync-based TCP transport. No HTTP/REST/gRPC (no tokio). Simple request-response framing.

5. **Failure modes** — Host goes offline, network partition, stale ownership, split-brain analysis.

6. **Design document** — docs/design-multi-host-repair.md

## Acceptance criteria
- Protocol design document with ownership model, conflict detection, exchange protocol
- Failure mode analysis with mitigations
- No distributed consensus required
- Transport uses asupersync exclusively
- Unit tests for ownership logic","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:41.578815960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:56:32.728825771Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T02:56:32.728142080Z","close_reason":"Completed: Protocol design doc at docs/design-multi-host-repair.md covering ownership model, conflict detection, symbol exchange wire format, and failure mode analysis (host crash, network partition, stale ownership, split-brain, clock skew). Implemented ffs-repair::ownership module with CoordinationRecord, RepairOwnership (try_acquire/renew/release/is_owned_by_us), OwnershipGuard, deterministic UUID tiebreak, atomic write-via-rename, TTL-based expiry. 15 unit tests pass. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","design","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:41.578815960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","title":"Implement lease-based repair ownership transfer","description":"## Purpose

Implement repair group ownership transfer between hosts with lease-based TTL and stale detection.

## Scope

1. **RepairOwnership record** — host_id, group_id, generation, acquired_at, ttl, lease_version.
2. **Acquire ownership** — Atomic compare-and-swap on coordination file. Fails if already owned by another host with valid lease.
3. **Renew lease** — Extend TTL before expiry. Fails if lease_version changed.
4. **Release ownership** — Explicit release or TTL expiry.
5. **Stale detection** — If owner fails to renew within TTL, any host can re-acquire.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ownership acquire/renew/release working
- Concurrent acquisition handled correctly (no split ownership)
- Stale detection after TTL expiry
- Unit tests for all ownership transitions
- E2E script with simulated multi-host ownership transfer","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:42.531302434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:14.874907878Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:42.531302434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:14.873800753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
